[{'sha': '83508eb193b722ef33fc649e0b90021b3b7097bb', 'message': "fix(git): respect core.commentChar when stripping commit message comments

The stripCommitMessageComments method hardcoded '#' as the comment character,
ignoring the user's core.commentChar git configuration. Users who set a custom
comment character (e.g., ';' or '*') would have comment lines from merge
messages, squash messages, and commit templates included in their commits
instead of being stripped.

Read core.commentChar from git config and use it for comment stripping.
Falls back to '#' when the config is not set, empty, or set to 'auto'.

Closes #133770"}]

commits:
[{'sha': 'ba512308db8a6150d5e3e198f3e94d32aca124a0', 'message': 'fix: avoid splitting surrogate pairs in computeMoreMinimalEdits

The `stringDiff` function in `$computeMoreMinimalEdits` operates on
UTF-16 code units, so when it minimizes edits between original and
replacement text containing emoji or other astral Unicode characters,
the diff boundaries can land between the high and low surrogate of a
surrogate pair. This produces corrupted edit text (e.g. replacing
"🌟 🌈 🚀 🎉" with "🎉 🚀 🌟 🌈" would yield "� 🚀 � 🌈").

Fix by adjusting each diff change boundary after computation: if it
splits a surrogate pair (high surrogate on one side, low surrogate on
the other), expand the change to include both halves.

Fixes #248268'}]

['`_pushHooks()` calls `this._promptsService.listPromptFiles(PromptsType.hook, ...)`, but hook-type prompt files currently won’t include API-provided hook files from the new `vscode.chat.registerHookProvider()` path. In `PromptsServiceImpl.getExtensionPromptFiles`, `PromptsType.hook` returns `undefined` from `getProviderActivationEvent`, so `listFromProviders(...)` is never invoked for hooks, and `invalidatePromptFileCache` also doesn’t handle `PromptsType.hook` (so caches/events won’t refresh when hook providers change). As a result, the new hook provider API will appear to register successfully but its hooks won’t surface via `chat.hooks`/`onDidChangeHooks`. Fix by adding a hook provider activation event (like the other provider types), wiring `PromptsType.hook` through `getProviderActivationEvent` + `listFromProviders`, and invalidating/refreshing the hook caches (`cachedFileLocations[PromptsType.hook]` and `cachedHooks`) when hook providers register/unregister or fire their change event.', '`onDidChangeHooks` is documented as “when hook files change”, but the implementation is currently derived from the cached hooks computation (which is also invalidated by config and workspace-trust changes) and it doesn’t get invalidated when hook prompt-file providers register/unregister or fire change events (because `invalidatePromptFileCache` has no `PromptsType.hook` branch). Either adjust the event’s contract to reflect that it fires when *effective hook availability/config* changes, or wire hook provider changes into the same invalidation path so the event accurately reflects hook resource list changes for the new `registerHookProvider` API.

review_comments:
['`_pushHooks()` calls `this._promptsService.listPromptFiles(PromptsType.hook, ...)`, but hook-type prompt files currently won’t include API-provided hook files from the new `vscode.chat.registerHookProvider()` path. In `PromptsServiceImpl.getExtensionPromptFiles`, `PromptsType.hook` returns `undefined` from `getProviderActivationEvent`, so `listFromProviders(...)` is never invoked for hooks, and `invalidatePromptFileCache` also doesn’t handle `PromptsType.hook` (so caches/events won’t refresh when hook providers change). As a result, the new hook provider API will appear to register successfully but its hooks won’t surface via `chat.hooks`/`onDidChangeHooks`. Fix by adding a hook provider activation event (like the other provider types), wiring `PromptsType.hook` through `getProviderActivationEvent` + `listFromProviders`, and invalidating/refreshing the hook caches (`cachedFileLocations[PromptsType.hook]` and `cachedHooks`) when hook providers register/unregister or fire their change event.', '`onDidChangeHooks` is documented as “when hook files change”, but the implementation is currently derived from the cached hooks computation (which is also invalidated by config and workspace-trust changes) and it doesn’t get invalidated when hook prompt-file providers register/unregister or fire change events (because `invalidatePromptFileCache` has no `PromptsType.hook` branch). Either adjust the event’s contract to reflect that it fires when *effective hook availability/config* changes, or wire hook provider changes into the same invalidation path so the event accurately reflects hook resource list changes for the new `registerHookProvider` API.
```suggestion
	 * Event that is triggered when the effective hook availability or configuration changes.
	 */
	readonly onDidChangeHooks: Event<void>;

	/**
	 * Gets all hooks collected from hooks.json files.
	 * The result is cached and invalidated when the effective hook availability or configuration changes.
```', '## Pull request overview

This PR extends the proposed `chatPromptFiles` API to include *hook configuration files* alongside agents, instructions, prompts, and skills, wiring hook discovery/updates through the same main-thread ↔ extension-host plumbing.

**Changes:**
- Adds a new proposed API surface for hooks (`ChatHookProvider`, `chat.hooks`, `chat.onDidChangeHooks`, `chat.registerHookProvider`).
- Extends the ext host protocol and ext host implementation to accept/publish hook resources (`IHookDto`, `$acceptHooks`, internal storage/eventing).
- Adds `IPromptsService.onDidChangeHooks` and wires main thread to push hooks to the extension host on change.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 7 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vscode-dts/vscode.proposed.chatPromptFiles.d.ts | Adds proposed API types/exports for hook providers and hook resources/events. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/test/common/promptSyntax/service/mockPromptsService.ts | Updates test mock to satisfy the new `IPromptsService` member. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/service/promptsServiceImpl.ts | Exposes `onDidChangeHooks` from the cached hooks computation. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/service/promptsService.ts | Adds `onDidChangeHooks` to `IPromptsService`. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostChatAgents2.ts | Stores hooks, fires `onDidChangeHooks`, and routes hook providers through the unified provider plumbing. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHost.protocol.ts | Adds `IHookDto` and `$acceptHooks` to the ext host shape. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHost.api.impl.ts | Exposes `chat.registerHookProvider`, `chat.hooks`, and `chat.onDidChangeHooks` to extensions. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadChatAgents2.ts | Pushes hook file resources to the extension host initially and when prompts-service hooks change. |
</details>




']

commits:
[{'sha': '73f8b755a42854e28fbc1d66e0c1efe4d5371558', 'message': "sessions: use newSessionResource directly to avoid duplicate session entry

When a new session is created, sendRequest() already returns the committed
URI as result.newSessionResource. Previously, both _sendFirstChat and
_sendSubsequentChat always awaited _waitForCommittedSession(), which listens
for a later onDidCommitSession IPC event. This created a window where both
the temp 'New Session...' entry and the real committed session were visible
simultaneously in the session list.

Now both methods use result.newSessionResource directly when available,
falling back to _waitForCommittedSession() only when it is not set. This
eliminates the duplicate entry flicker.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"}]
review_comments:
["There’s no unit test coverage for this new fast-path. Consider adding a test that stubs `chatService.sendRequest` to return `kind: 'sent'` with `newSessionResource` set, and keeps `chatSessionsService.onDidCommitSession` silent, then asserts `_sendFirstChat` still completes and replaces the temp session without waiting on the commit event (i.e., doesn’t hang).

This issue also appears on line 1471 of the same file.", "## Pull request overview

This PR fixes a transient duplicate entry in the Sessions list when creating a new agent session and sending the first message, by avoiding an unnecessary async wait for the commit event when the committed resource URI is already available from `sendRequest()`.

**Changes:**
- Use `result.newSessionResource` immediately (when present) to determine the committed session URI in `_sendFirstChat`.
- Apply the same committed-URI fast-path in `_sendSubsequentChat`, falling back to waiting for `onDidCommitSession` only when needed.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts:1474**
* Similar to `_sendFirstChat`, this updated committed-URI selection in `_sendSubsequentChat` isn’t covered by tests. Please add a test that returns `newSessionResource` from `sendRequest` and verifies the method completes without relying on `onDidCommitSession`, and that the temp chat ID is replaced in the group model/session cache as expected.
```
			// Use the committed URI directly if available, otherwise fall back to waiting for the IPC event
			const committedResource = (result.kind === 'sent' && result.newSessionResource)
				? result.newSessionResource
				: await this._waitForCommittedSession(newChatSession.resource);
```
</details>

"]

Consider returning base64 by default (or honoring the requested `encoding`), and only using UTF-8 when you can guarantee the resource is text.

```', "## Pull request overview

This PR adopts updates from the Agent Host Protocol (AHP) to improve file edit modeling (supporting create/delete/rename in addition to content edits) and replaces the older `browseDirectory`/`fetchContent`/`writeFile` surface with generalized resource operations (`resourceList`/`resourceRead`/`resourceWrite`/`resourceDelete`/`resourceMove`/`resourceCopy`) across the agent host stack.

**Changes:**
- Update protocol command surface to resource-based filesystem operations and plumb through client/server handlers.
- Switch file edit tool-result schema from `beforeURI`/`afterURI` to structured `before`/`after` objects and introduce `FileEditKind`.
- Update agent-host editing session undo/redo to operate via filesystem operations (including create/delete/rename) and migrate session DB schema accordingly.

Consider restricting this fallback to `resources[0].scheme === Schemas.file` (or equivalent) and otherwise using `resources[0].toString()` (and ideally add a small test for a non-file URI).', 'Resolved: [81257dd](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/pull/307501/commits/81257dda607539e137915bb2c9c430b3733d9dd6)', '## Pull request overview

This PR improves terminal paste behavior by falling back to clipboard “resources” (e.g. copied files) when the clipboard has no text, enabling pasting a file path into the integrated terminal.

**Changes:**
- Update `TerminalClipboardContribution.paste()` to use the first clipboard resource’s path when `readText()` is empty.
- Add unit tests covering text paste, resource fallback, and empty clipboard behavior.

']

review_comments:
['## Pull request overview

This PR addresses a sessions-app-specific UX issue by hiding chat input picker controls when they’re disabled/unavailable, and by limiting certain repository configuration pickers to the “new session” state.

**Changes:**
- Update sessions pickers (mode/model/isolation/branch/session-type) to hide their slots when unavailable and remove them from keyboard navigation.
- Restrict repository-config picker action contributions (worktree/isolation + branch) to `IsNewChatSessionContext`.
- Add a focused browser test ensuring the session-type picker is hidden when only one session type is available, and add scoped Sessions CSS to hide shared workbench pickers in the active chat UI.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/modePicker.ts | Hide the mode picker slot when only one mode is available; update tab focus/ARIA accordingly. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/modelPicker.ts | Hide the cloud model picker slot when no models are available; update tab focus/ARIA accordingly. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/isolationPicker.ts | Hide the isolation picker when not applicable (config disabled or no git repo); update tab focus/ARIA accordingly. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/branchPicker.ts | Hide the branch picker while loading or when not applicable; update tab focus/ARIA accordingly. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsActions.ts | Gate isolation/branch picker contributions behind `IsNewChatSessionContext` to avoid showing them outside new-session state. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/sessionTypePicker.ts | Hide the session-type picker when only one session type is available; update tab focus/ARIA accordingly. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/test/browser/sessionTypePicker.test.ts | Add coverage for hiding/showing the session-type picker based on available session types. |
| src/vs/sessions/browser/media/style.css | Add scoped Sessions CSS to hide shared workbench chat session pickers in the sessions app active chat UI. |
</details>




', 'i think this looks good, thanks!']

Consider moving this check outside the `ctrlCmd` block (or broadening the outer condition) so `Ctrl+Space` can pass through to the browser/OS.', '## Pull request overview

This PR adjusts Integrated Browser keydown forwarding so that more native OS/browser editing shortcuts (notably `Cmd+Arrow` on macOS) are not intercepted and forwarded to the VS Code workbench, addressing the regression reported in #307355.

**Changes:**
- Introduces a platform-specific allowlist of Ctrl/Cmd-based native editing shortcuts that should not be forwarded.
- Ensures plain modifier-only key presses (Ctrl/Shift/Alt/Meta) are never treated as forwardable keybindings.
- Extends the “let the browser handle it” logic beyond copy/paste/undo to include navigation keys (e.g., arrows/home/end) when used with Ctrl/Cmd.



', '## 📬 CODENOTIFY

The following users are being notified based on files changed in this PR:

### @jruales

Matched files:
- `src/vs/platform/browserView/electron-browser/preload-browserView.ts`
']

Consider changing the input/expectation to use exactly one trailing backslash so the test validates the real-world case.

```', 'This suite no longer has coverage for the documented fallback behavior when no shell is available (the parenthesized `(TMPDIR=...; export TMPDIR; ...)` form). Since `_wrapUnsandboxedCommand` retains this branch, adding a focused assertion that `wrapCommand(..., true, /* shell */ undefined)` (or empty) still uses the fallback would help prevent regressions.', '## Pull request overview

This PR updates the *unsandboxed* terminal sandbox wrapping path to avoid syntactic breakage when commands contain tricky shell characters (notably trailing backslashes), by executing the full command via the active shell using `-c` rather than interpolating raw text into a parenthesized wrapper.

**Changes:**
- Plumb the active shell through the command-line sandbox rewriter into `TerminalSandboxService.wrapCommand`.
- Extend `ITerminalSandboxService.wrapCommand`/`TerminalSandboxService.wrapCommand` to accept an optional `shell` for unsandboxed wrapping.
- Switch unsandboxed wrapping to `env TMPDIR="..." <shell> -c '<command>'` (with a parenthesized fallback when no shell is provided), and add/adjust regression tests.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/test/browser/terminalSandboxService.test.ts | Updates/extends regression tests to validate the new unsandboxed wrapping format and blocked-domain unsandboxed behavior. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/common/terminalSandboxService.ts | Adds an optional `shell` parameter and implements the new unsandboxed wrapping strategy using `env … <shell> -c …`. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/commandLineRewriter/commandLineSandboxRewriter.ts | Forwards the active shell into `wrapCommand` so unsandboxed wrapping can use the correct shell. |



']

review_comments:
['`_restoreFocusIfRequested` can consume the pending focus request and call `focus()` even when the target toggle is not actually focusable/visible yet. In particular, the titlebar copy of the toggle lives under `.part.titlebar` and can be `display: none` while the sidebar is visible (see `src/vs/sessions/browser/parts/media/titlebarpart.css`). If focus is attempted while hidden, `ActionViewItem.focus()` is a no-op, but the request is still consumed, so focus won’t be restored later. Consider gating the titlebar focus path on the sidebar actually being hidden (or verifying the element is focusable/visible) and/or only consuming the request immediately before a successful focus attempt.', 'This introduces new stateful behavior (request/consume/clear) that is easy to regress and currently has no direct unit coverage. Consider adding a small browser-unit test validating the focus-request lifecycle (request → consume only for matching target → cleared) and that `clearSidebarToggleFocusRequest` prevents later consumption.', '## Pull request overview

This PR improves keyboard accessibility in the Agent Sessions window by preserving focus on the “Toggle Primary Side Bar Visibility” control as it moves between the titlebar and sidebar UI surfaces when the primary sidebar is shown/hidden.

**Changes:**
- Documented the new focus-preservation behavior for the sessions sidebar toggle.
- Added a small shared focus-request utility (`request/consume/clear`) to coordinate focus handoff.
- Updated the custom sidebar-toggle `ActionViewItem` to restore focus across sidebar visibility transitions (including when the sidebar view container opens).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| `src/vs/sessions/LAYOUT.md` | Updates documentation to reflect focus handoff behavior for the sidebar toggle. |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/sessions/browser/sessionsTitleBarWidget.ts` | Adds focus-restoration logic to the custom sidebar toggle view item and updates icon class selection. |
| `src/vs/sessions/browser/sidebarToggleFocus.ts` | Introduces shared state/functions for requesting and consuming a pending focus handoff. |
| `src/vs/sessions/browser/layoutActions.ts` | Requests/clears focus handoff when toggling the sidebar from the toggle control; updates open/closed icons. |



']

review_comments:
['`packErrorForTelemetry` can throw when called with `null`/`undefined` (or other falsy values) because it unconditionally reads `err.stack`/`err.message`. Since this helper is used from `catch` blocks where `throw undefined` / non-Error values are possible, please guard `err` first (e.g., treat non-object values as having no stack/message) so telemetry logging never throws while handling an error.
```suggestion
	// Guard against non-object or falsy values (e.g. `throw undefined`, `throw "error"`).
	if (!err || (typeof err !== 'object' && typeof err !== 'function')) {
		return {
			callstack: undefined,
			msg: safeStringify(err)
		};
	}

	const errorLike = err as { stack?: unknown; message?: unknown };
	const rawStack = Array.isArray(errorLike.stack) ? errorLike.stack.join('\n') : errorLike.stack;
	const callstack: string | undefined = typeof rawStack === 'string' ? rawStack : undefined;

	const msg = typeof errorLike.message === 'string' && errorLike.message
		? errorLike.message
		: safeStringify(err);
```', 'Adding `callstack` to `IChatResponseErrorDetails` makes it part of the normal chat response model and serialization/export pipeline. That contradicts the comment (“only sent via telemetry”) and can inadvertently persist/export potentially sensitive stack data (paths, usernames) and increase stored session size. Consider keeping the callstack telemetry-only (e.g., strip it before storing the response / before serialization, or transport it via a separate transient field not persisted/exported).
```suggestion

```', 'In the `catch` path, `errorAgent` is derived from `agentPart?.agent ?? defaultAgent`, but the request may have been re-scoped via participant detection (`detectedAgent`). This can lead to incorrect `agent` / `agentExtensionId` values in `chatAgentError` telemetry for detected participants. Use the same agent selection as the other error path (e.g., `detectedAgent ?? agentPart?.agent ?? defaultAgent`).
```suggestion
						const errorAgent = detectedAgent ?? agentPart?.agent ?? defaultAgent;
```', 'The `chatAgentError` event type + classification are duplicated in two blocks. This increases the chance of them drifting (e.g., one path adds a property and the other doesn’t). Consider hoisting the type/classification to a shared scope (file-level or a small helper like `logChatAgentError(...)`) and reuse it from both paths.', 'Fixed — added a guard for non-object/falsy values at the top of `packErrorForTelemetry`. Falls back to `{ callstack: undefined, msg: safeStringify(err) }` for `null`, `undefined`, strings, numbers, etc.', 'Good catch — the callstack was indeed getting serialized via `ChatResponseModel.toJSON()` → `result.errorDetails`. Fixed by adding `delete rawResult.errorDetails.callstack` after emitting the telemetry event, before calling `model.setResponse()`.', 'Fixed — now uses `detectedAgent ?? agentPart?.agent ?? defaultAgent` to match the other error path.', 'Fixed — extracted a file-level `logChatAgentError(telemetryService, agent, callstack, msg)` helper that owns the event type and GDPR classification. Both call sites now use it.', '## Pull request overview

This PR improves diagnostics for chat agent failures by transporting error callstacks from the extension host to the workbench and emitting structured error telemetry, while also adding a generic `isError` tag to all error-level telemetry events.

**Changes:**
- Added `packErrorForTelemetry()` helper and reused it in `BaseErrorTelemetry`.
- Transported chat agent callstacks across the RPC boundary and logged a new `chatAgentError` telemetry event with callstack + agent metadata.
- Tagged all `TelemetryLevel.ERROR` events with `isError: true` for backend-side identification.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/platform/telemetry/common/errorTelemetry.ts | Adds reusable error-to-telemetry packing helper and reuses it in base error telemetry. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatService.ts | Extends chat error details shape to optionally carry a callstack. |
| src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostChatAgents2.ts | Captures callstack when agent invocation throws and includes it in returned error details. |
| src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/chatService/chatServiceImpl.ts | Emits `chatAgentError` telemetry (agent-returned error + outer catch path). |
| src/vs/platform/telemetry/common/telemetryService.ts | Adds a generic `isError` marker to error-level events before appending. |
</details>




']

**Changes:**
- Allow `Win32UpdateService.doQuitAndInstall()` to proceed when the update state is `Restarting` (in addition to `Ready`).



']

commits: [{'sha': '3cb58f4796a0efb90847be511a03ec2b37251330', 'message': 'Sessions - update context keys for actions'}]
review_comments:
["This adds another hard-coded context key string (`'sessions.hasUncommittedChanges'`). In the sessions codebase there’s an emerging convention to centralize context keys as `RawContextKey` exports and reference them via `.key` (e.g. `src/vs/sessions/common/contextkeys.ts`). To avoid typos and make refactors safer, consider defining these `sessions.has*` keys in a shared module (and reusing the same constant in `changesView.ts` where the key is created) and using `ContextKeyExpr.equals(<keyConst>.key, …)` here.", '## Pull request overview

Updates Sessions window action visibility by refining Git-related context keys so actions (notably “Mark as Done”) only appear in appropriate repository states.

**Changes:**
- Tighten “Mark as Done” action visibility to require no uncommitted changes.
- Simplify repository-state derived computations in the Changes view by removing redundant guards.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/sessions/browser/views/sessionsViewActions.ts | Adds a new context-key constraint for showing “Mark as Done” when the repo is clean. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts | Adjusts how outgoing/uncommitted-change context keys are computed from repository state. |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts:934**
* `hasUncommittedChangesContextKey` now returns `false` when `repositoryState` is undefined, which can happen while `isLoadingChangesObs` is true (repo path known but repository not opened yet). That makes the toolbar conditions treat the repo as “clean” during loading and can briefly enable/show actions that require `sessions.hasUncommittedChanges === false` (e.g. “Mark as Done”), causing flicker and potentially incorrect action availability. Consider restoring the previous conservative behavior for the loading/undefined-state case (e.g. keep returning `true` when `hasGitRepository` but `repositoryState` is missing, or gate on `isLoadingChangesObs`/use a last-known value).
```
			this.renderDisposables.add(bindContextKey(hasUncommittedChangesContextKey, this.scopedContextKeyService, reader => {
				const repository = this.viewModel.activeSessionRepositoryObs.read(reader);
				const repositoryState = repository?.state.read(reader);

				return (repositoryState?.mergeChanges.length ?? 0) > 0 ||
					(repositoryState?.indexChanges.length ?? 0) > 0 ||
					(repositoryState?.workingTreeChanges.length ?? 0) > 0 ||
					(repositoryState?.untrackedChanges.length ?? 0) > 0;
```
</details>

']

To avoid typos and make refactors safer, consider defining these `sessions.has*` keys in a shared module (and reusing the same constant in `changesView.ts` where the key is created) and using `ContextKeyExpr.equals(<keyConst>.key, …)` here.", '## Pull request overview

Updates Sessions window action visibility by refining Git-related context keys so actions (notably “Mark as Done”) only appear in appropriate repository states.

**Changes:**
- Tighten “Mark as Done” action visibility to require no uncommitted changes.
- Simplify repository-state derived computations in the Changes view by removing redundant guards.

| File | Description |

<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts:934**
* `hasUncommittedChangesContextKey` now returns `false` when `repositoryState` is undefined, which can happen while `isLoadingChangesObs` is true (repo path known but repository not opened yet). That makes the toolbar conditions treat the repo as “clean” during loading and can briefly enable/show actions that require `sessions.hasUncommittedChanges === false` (e.g. “Mark as Done”), causing flicker and potentially incorrect action availability. Consider restoring the previous conservative behavior for the loading/undefined-state case (e.g. keep returning `true` when `hasGitRepository` but `repositoryState` is missing, or gate on `isLoadingChangesObs`/use a last-known value).
```
			this.renderDisposables.add(bindContextKey(hasUncommittedChangesContextKey, this.scopedContextKeyService, reader => {

					(repositoryState?.untrackedChanges.length ?? 0) > 0;
```

['## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the Sessions “PR checks” UI by extracting CI-check plumbing into a dedicated `ChecksViewModel`, renaming/realigning the widget assets, and introducing a workbench action + context key for “Fix CI Checks”.

**Changes:**
- Replace ad-hoc CI model wiring in `ChangesViewPane` with a reusable `ChecksViewModel`.
- Add `checksActions.ts` to provide shared CI-check utilities, a failed-checks context key, and a “Fix CI Checks” action.
- Minor UI tweaks (badge/welcome initial visibility, checks widget CSS spacing, updated aria-label text).

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/media/checksWidget.css | Adds right padding for the per-check action area. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/checksWidget.ts | Renames CSS import and switches widget binding API to `setInput(ChecksViewModel)`. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/checksViewModel.ts | New view model to derive active session resource + CI model, and manage refresh/polling. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/checksActions.ts | New shared CI utilities + failed-checks context contribution + “Fix CI Checks” action. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.ts | Uses `ChecksViewModel` for the checks pane; adjusts initial visibility for some UI elements. |
| src/vs/sessions/contrib/changes/browser/changesView.contribution.ts | Switches side-effect import from old CI action to `checksActions`. |
</details>




']

["`spawn` is always passing a 5th argument for the sibling exe name, but when `productService.win32SiblingExeBasename` is undefined it becomes an empty string (`''`). That means `inno_updater.exe` will receive an extra empty CLI argument, which is inconsistent with the Inno Setup invocation (it omits the argument entirely when not needed) and can break argument parsing.

Consider building the argv array conditionally so the sibling exe argument is only included when `win32SiblingExeBasename` is set (and optionally normalizing to avoid accidental double “.exe” suffixes).", 'inno updater can handle empty or undefined argument', 'https://github.com/microsoft/inno-updater/blob/05beec77b463c0ae4f06d50ccb850b44a8eba0ff/src/main.rs#L1271-L1272', '## Pull request overview

This PR addresses Windows agent launch failures after updates by ensuring background-update garbage collection (GC) preserves additional “sibling” executables (e.g., an embedded/proxy exe) instead of cleaning them up.

**Changes:**
- Extend Win32 background-update GC invocation to pass the main exe name plus an optional sibling exe name to `inno_updater.exe`.
- Add `win32SiblingExeBasename` to product configuration / embedded product typing so the sibling exe can be specified via product metadata.
- Update the Inno Setup script to forward the optional proxy exe name to `inno_updater.exe` (both during background update and GC).

| build/win32/code.iss | Forwards optional `ProxyExeBasename` to `inno_updater.exe` for background update and GC. |
| build/lib/embeddedType.ts | Extends embedded product info typing with optional `win32SiblingExeBasename`. |
